content.problemStatement:
Hulk has a set of distinct points PPP in the 2D plane. His signature move is HulkHulkHulk SmashSmashSmash. He performed HulkHulkHulk SmashSmashSmash on the set PPP which caused the points to change, and so he obtained a new set of points PвЂІP'PвЂІ.

More formally, HulkHulkHulk SmashSmashSmash makes the new set PвЂІP'PвЂІ in the following way:


Initially, let PвЂІP'PвЂІ be empty.
For every PiP_iPiвЂ‹ and PjP_jPjвЂ‹ such that 1в‰¤i<jв‰¤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1в‰¤i<jв‰¤N , add the mid point of PiP_iPiвЂ‹ and PjP_jPjвЂ‹ to the set PвЂІP'PвЂІ (only if it didn't exist in it already).
So, PвЂІP'PвЂІ is the set of midpoints of line segments connecting pairs of distinct points of PPP.
Note that points from PPP do not necessarily have to be in PвЂІP'PвЂІ. See the samples below for a graphical example.

Find the number of points of PвЂІP'PвЂІ which lie on the convex hull of PвЂІP'PвЂІ. A point is said to lie on the convex hull if the shape of the convex hull is changed when this point is removed.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the size of PPP.
The next NNN lines contain two space-separated integers each, where the iii-th line contains Pi.x,Pi.yP_i.x,P_i.yPiвЂ‹.x,PiвЂ‹.y denoting the x-coordinate and y-coordinate of the iii-th point.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the number of points of PвЂІP'PвЂІ which lie on its convex hull.
constraints: The convex hull of the input set PPP has positive area
Sum of NNN over all tests cases does not exceed 2в‹…1052 \cdot 10^52в‹…105
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103

In the below images, the set of points PPP is denoted by the color pink, the set of points PвЂІP'PвЂІ is denoted by the color green. The blue point is the origin.


The below image represents the first test case





The below image represents the second test case





Note that by our definition, points (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4) and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4) are not considered to lie on the convex hull of PвЂІP'PвЂІ, because removing one of them does not change the shape of the hull.
